name: emcee (MCMC Ensemble Sampler)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Dan Foreman-Mackey / Flatiron Institute

performance.notes: Python with optional MPI parallelism; MCMC steps CPU-bound; ~10K steps typical

agent_readiness.notes: Affine-invariant MCMC ensemble sampler. import emcee. def log_prob(theta): return log_likelihood + log_prior. sampler = emcee.EnsembleSampler(nwalkers=32, ndim=5, log_prob_fn=log_prob). sampler.run_mcmc(initial_state, nsteps=5000, progress=True). flat_samples = sampler.get_chain(discard=100, thin=15, flat=True). sampler.get_log_prob(). corner.corner(flat_samples) for visualization. Auto-correlation: sampler.get_autocorr_time(). MPI: from emcee.utils import MPIPool. H5 backend: emcee.backends.HDFBackend('chain.h5').

gotchas: Ensemble sampler assumes unimodal posteriors — multimodal distributions are problematic
Convergence must be assessed manually (autocorrelation time)
Number of walkers must be > 2 * ndim (recommend 4-8x)
Burn-in (discard) period must be chosen carefully
best_when: Bayesian parameter estimation for model fitting
Astronomical data fitting (the most cited MCMC package in astronomy)
When posterior distributions are needed alongside maximum likelihood
Parallelized MCMC with MPI for HPC clusters
avoid_when: Strongly multimodal posteriors (use nested sampling: dynesty, MultiNest)
When PyMC or Stan's variational inference is faster
summary: emcee provides an affine-invariant MCMC ensemble sampler. EnsembleSampler(nwalkers=32, ndim=5, log_prob_fn=log_prob).run_mcmc(initial, nsteps=5000). get_chain(discard=100, flat=True). Autocorrelation convergence. HDF5 backend. MPI parallelism. MIT by Dan Foreman-Mackey. The most cited astronomy MCMC package (>15K citations). Best for Bayesian model fitting with ~2-50 parameters. Ensemble sampler struggles with multimodal posteriors.
